heading: Terrain
content: Dungeon maps use three terrain types:

heading: Floor (Normal)
content: Standard passable squares. No restrictions.

heading: Water
content: - Most units CANNOT step onto water squares. - Knights cannot land on water but can leap over it. - Water blocks diagonal slides for Fire Elemental, Troll, Demonics, and Shaman. - Cannon lines skip ov

heading: Void
content: - Off-board squares. Cannot be entered by any unit. - Creates the dungeon's corridors and chokepoints. - All sliding pieces stop at void boundaries — voids block slides for every unit equally. Draftin

heading: Army Drafting
content: Before each battle, players build their army from an XP budget. Budget: 75 XP Constraints: - Must include exactly 1 King - Must include at least 1 Pawn - No maximum team size — as many units as budget

heading: Setup
content: 1. Choose your faction 2. Draft your army 75 XP 3. Choose turn order: You First, AI First, or Random 4. Deploy units in your spawn zone bottom rows of the map

heading: Turn Order
content: - 2-player: alternating turns - 4-player: cyclic Player → AI 1 → AI 2 → AI 3 - Each turn: move one unit OR attack with one unit

heading: Check
content: - A King is in check if an enemy unit could capture it on the next move. - You CANNOT make a move that leaves your own King in check. - You MUST resolve check on your turn move King, block, or capture

heading: Win Condition
content: Capture the enemy King. The game ends immediately when a King is taken. In 4-player games, eliminated players' remaining pieces are removed; the last King standing wins.

heading: Undo
content: Players may undo their last move reverting both their move and the AI's response. Maps

heading: Almost Chess
content: One Queen is replaced by a Chancellor Rook + Knight compound. "Almost" standard chess — one piece different, but the implications ripple through the entire game.

heading: Setup
content: Board: Standard 8×8. Setup FEN: rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBCKBNR White: Rook, Knight, Bishop, Chancellor, King, Bishop, Knight, Rook + 8 pawns. Black: Standard setup with Queen.

heading: Pieces
content: Pieces: - Chancellor C — combines Rook and Knight movement. Can slide along ranks/files like a Rook OR jump in L-shapes like a Knight. Placed on d1 where White's Queen normally sits.

heading: Rules
content: All standard chess rules apply. The Chancellor replaces White's Queen only. Black retains their standard Queen.

heading: Win Condition
content: Checkmate, same as standard chess.

heading: Strategy
content: The Chancellor is slightly weaker than a Queen no diagonal sliding but has the Knight's forking ability combined with Rook-length reach. White lacks diagonal firepower from d1 and must rely more on Bi

heading: Attribution
content: Ralph Betza, 1977. Public domain.

heading: Amazon Chess
content: Both Queens are replaced by Amazons — the most powerful piece possible, combining Queen and Knight movement.

heading: Setup
content: Board: Standard 8×8. Setup FEN: rnbmkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBMKBNR Both sides: Rook, Knight, Bishop, Amazon, King, Bishop, Knight, Rook + 8 pawns.

heading: Pieces
content: Pieces: - Amazon M — combines Queen and Knight movement. Can slide along ranks, files, and diagonals like a Queen OR jump in L-shapes like a Knight. The most powerful piece in any chess variant.

heading: Rules
content: All standard chess rules apply. Amazons replace Queens for both sides symmetrically.

heading: Win Condition
content: Checkmate, same as standard chess.

heading: Strategy
content: The Amazon is devastatingly powerful — it controls more squares than any other piece and cannot be blocked by interposing. Games tend to be shorter and more tactical because the Amazon's range makes d

heading: Attribution
content: Traditional fairy chess piece. Variant is public domain.

heading: Andernach Chess
content: When a piece captures, it switches sides — the capturing piece becomes the opponent's piece on the captured square. Kings are exempt from colour change.

content: Board: Standard 8×8. Setup: Standard chess starting position. FEN: rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 0 1

heading: Rules
content: - All pieces move as in standard chess. - When a piece captures an enemy piece, the captured piece is removed and the capturing piece changes colour becomes the opponent's piece. - Kings are exempt —

heading: Win Condition
content: Checkmate standard.

heading: Strategy
content: Every capture is double-edged — you remove an enemy piece but hand them the capturer. Material advantages are nearly impossible to build through captures alone. Non-capturing moves pawn advances, posi

heading: Attribution
content: Created for a chess composition event in Andernach, Germany, 1993. Public domain rules.

heading: Antichess
content: Captures are mandatory. Lose all your pieces to win. Inverts the entire logic of chess.

heading: Setup
content: Board: Standard 8×8. Setup: Standard chess setup. FEN: rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 0 1

heading: Rules
content: - If a capture is available, you MUST capture. If multiple captures are available, you may choose which one. - The King has no royal status — it can be captured like any other piece and there is no ch

heading: Win Condition
content: Lose all your pieces, or be stalemated.

heading: Strategy
content: You want to force your opponent to capture your pieces while avoiding being forced to keep yours. Opening theory is highly developed — certain openings force wins within a few moves.

heading: Attribution
content: Also known as "Losing Chess," "Giveaway Chess," or "Suicide Chess." Traditional variant, 19th century. Public domain.

heading: Atomic Chess
content: Captures explode, destroying all non-pawn pieces in a one-square radius around the capture square. Including the capturing piece.

content: Board: Standard 8×8. Setup: Standard chess setup. FEN: rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 0 1

content: - When a piece captures, an "explosion" occurs on the capture square. - The explosion destroys: the capturing piece, the captured piece, and ALL non-pawn pieces on squares adjacent horizontally, verti

heading: Win Condition
content: Destroy the opponent's King by exploding it or, rarely, checkmate.

heading: Strategy
content: Material sacrifices that trigger chain explosions near the enemy King are the primary tactic. Early Queen sacrifices are common if they explode near the King. Pawn structure matters because pawns are

heading: Attribution
content: Traditional variant. Popularised on Lichess and Chess.com. Public domain.

heading: Benedict Chess
content: There are no captures. When a piece moves to a square from which it attacks enemy pieces, those pieces convert to the mover's colour. Win by converting the opponent's King.

heading: Setup
content: Board: Standard 8×8. Setup: Standard chess starting position. FEN: rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 0 1

heading: Rules
content: - Pieces move as in standard chess but there are NO captures. - When a piece moves to a new square, every enemy piece it now attacks could move to, if it were a capture immediately converts to the mov

heading: Win Condition
content: Convert the opponent's King to your colour by moving a piece that attacks the King's square.

heading: Strategy
content: Long-range pieces Queen, Bishops, Rooks are enormously powerful because they can convert many pieces at once. The game snowballs — converting pieces gives you more attackers. Protecting your King from

heading: Attribution
content: C. S. Elliott, 1971. Named after Benedict Arnold. Public domain rules.

heading: Berolina Chess
content: Pawns are inverted: they advance diagonally and capture straight ahead. Named after Berlin, where it was invented in 1926.

content: Board: Standard 8×8. Setup: Standard chess setup pawns use Berolina movement rules. FEN: rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 0 1

heading: Win Condition
content: Checkmate the opponent's King.

heading: Strategy
content: Berolina pawns control different squares than normal pawns. A pawn on e2 can advance to d3 or f3 and double-move to c4 or g4, but captures on e3. This makes pawn chains behave very differently — adjac

heading: Attribution
content: Invented by Edmund Hebermann in Berlin, 1926. Public domain.

heading: Berserk Chess
content: When you deliver check, you immediately get a bonus move with a different piece. Aggressive play is rewarded with extra tempo.

content: Board: Standard 8×8. Setup: Standard chess starting position. FEN: rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 0 1

content: - Standard chess movement applies. - When a player delivers check, they immediately get one bonus move. - The bonus move must be made by a DIFFERENT piece than the one that gave check. - The bonus mov

content: Checkmate standard.

content: Discovered checks are extremely powerful — the discovering piece delivers check, and the bonus move can develop or reposition another piece. Queen and Bishop batteries that create check threats domina

heading: Attribution
content: Modern variant, popularised on online chess platforms 2000s. Public domain rules.

heading: Breakthrough
content: Pawns only. First to reach the far rank wins. A minimalist abstract strategy game.

content: First player to reach the opponent's back rank rank 7 for White, rank 1 for Black wins. If a player has no legal moves, they lose.

heading: Checkless Chess
content: You may not give check unless that check is also checkmate. The King becomes a bold attacker since it cannot be casually harassed.

heading: Setup
content: Board: Standard 8×8. Setup: Standard chess starting position. FEN: rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 0 1

heading: Win Condition
content: Checkmate — the only legal form of check.

heading: Attribution
content: Traditional variant. Earliest documented reference in Max Lange, 1857. Public domain.

heading: Chigorin
content: White replaces both Bishops with Knights. Four Knights vs two Knights and two Bishops — a fundamental asymmetry that changes positional evaluation.

content: Board: Standard 8×8. Setup FEN: rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNNQKNNR White: Rook, Knight, Knight, Queen, King, Knight, Knight, Rook + 8 pawns. Black: Standard setup.

heading: Rules
content: All standard chess rules apply. Castling is available for both sides.

heading: Win Condition
content: Checkmate, same as standard chess.

heading: Attribution
content: Named after Mikhail Chigorin. Variant design is traditional. Public domain.

heading: Codrus
content: Sacrifice your King to win. There is no concept of check — the King can walk into danger and must be deliberately captured. Named after King Codrus of Athens, who sacrificed himself to save his city.

content: Board: Standard 8×8. Setup: Standard chess starting position. FEN: rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 0 1

heading: Dice Chess

heading: Progressive
content: Each turn, the number of moves you make escalates: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5... Delivering check ends your turn early regardless of how many moves remain.

heading: Racing Kings
content: No checks allowed. Race your King to rank 8. A pure positional race with no violence.

heading: Setup
content: Board: Standard 8×8. Setup: All pieces start on ranks 1–2 in a specific non-standard arrangement. No pawns. FEN: 8/8/8/8/8/8/krbnNBRK/qrbnNBRQ w - - 0 1

heading: Rules
content: - No move may give check to either King. Any move that would place either King in check is illegal. - No castling. No pawns. No pawn promotion.

heading: Win Condition
content: First player to move their King to rank 8 wins. If White reaches rank 8, Black gets one more move — if Black also reaches rank 8, the game is a draw.

heading: Strategy
content: Block your opponent's King path while clearing your own. Rooks and Queens control files; Bishops control diagonals. The constraint against giving check makes many "obvious" blocking moves illegal.

heading: Attribution

heading: Shatar (Mongolian Chess)

heading: Stalemate Wins
content: Standard chess with one rule change: stalemating your opponent wins the game instead of drawing. This eliminates all stalemate-based drawing resources.

heading: Three-Check
content: Deliver three checks to win. No checkmate needed. Completely changes the value of forcing moves.

heading: Win Condition
content: - Checkmate as normal, OR - Deliver check three times. Each check is counted regardless of whether it was blocked, evaded, or interposed.

heading: Toroidal Chess
content: The board wraps in both directions — files connect left-to-right AND ranks connect top-to-bottom, forming a torus. There are no edges.
